_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A1/005 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 005 · A Day at the Farm

npc.text: I went to a farm last weekend.

routes.meaning: Anna says it sounds fun and asks what Tom saw.
examples: That sounds fun! What did you see?
That sounds fun! What did you see there?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds fun! What did you see?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks where the farm is.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds fun! What did you see?”

partial.message: React to Tom: “That sounds fun! What did you see?”

off_topic.message: Tom went to a farm. Say “That sounds fun! What did you see?”

npc.text: It's near my grandma's house. I saw many animals!

routes.meaning: Anna asks what animals Tom saw.

off_topic.message: Tom saw many animals. Ask “What did you see?”

npc.text: I saw cows, pigs, and chickens.

routes.meaning: Anna asks if Tom fed the animals.
examples: Did you feed the farm animals?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you feed the animals?”

routes.meaning: Anna says she likes one of the animals.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you feed the animals?”

partial.message: Ask Tom: “Did you feed the animals?”

off_topic.message: Tom saw cows, pigs, and chickens. Ask “Did you feed the animals?”

npc.text: The pigs were so cute! I fed some animals too.

routes.meaning: Anna asks if Tom fed the animals.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you feed the animals?”

partial.message: Ask Tom: “Did you feed the animals?”

off_topic.message: Tom fed some animals. Ask “Did you feed the animals?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks what the cows did.
examples: What did the cows do all day?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What did the cows do?”

routes.meaning: Anna says feeding chickens is fun.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What did the cows do?”

partial.message: Ask Tom: “What did the cows do?”

off_topic.message: Tom fed the chickens. Ask “What did the cows do?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks if Tom rode a horse.
examples: Did you ride a horse there?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you ride a horse?”

routes.meaning: Anna is surprised the cows ate all day.
examples: That is a lot of grass!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Did you ride a horse?”

partial.message: Ask Tom: “Did you ride a horse?”

off_topic.message: The cows ate grass. Ask “Did you ride a horse?”

npc.text: Yes, I rode a brown horse.

learning_objective: Say what you want to do

routes.meaning: Anna says she wants to visit a farm too.
examples: I want to visit a farm, too!
I want to go to a farm!
I want to see the animals too!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I want to visit a farm, too!”

routes.meaning: Anna asks if the horse was fast.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I want to visit a farm, too!”

partial.message: Tell Tom: “I want to visit a farm, too!”

off_topic.message: Tom rode a brown horse. Say “I want to visit a farm, too!”

npc.text: Yes, a little! It was a fun ride.

routes.meaning: Anna says she wants to visit a farm too.
examples: I want to visit a farm, too!
I want to go to a farm!
I want to see the animals!
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I want to visit a farm, too!”

partial.message: Tell Tom: “I want to visit a farm, too!”

off_topic.message: The ride was fun. Say “I want to visit a farm, too!”

npc.text: You should! Let's go to the farm together next time.
